concerns.text: FR-004: '...product of random transfer matrices [deferred] iterations'. The number of iterations is a critical parameter for the Lyapunov exponent calculation. Using '[deferred]' here makes the requirement unverifiable; the system cannot be tested without a specific iteration count or a defined convergence criterion (e.g., 'until |γ_n - γ_{n-1}| < 1e-4').

FR-009: '...logarithmic accumulation... [deferred] iterations'. Similar to FR-004, the scope of the accumulation is undefined. Without a specific iteration count or stopping condition, the implementation is ambiguous and the success of the 'prevention of underflow' cannot be measured.

Acceptance Scenario 1: '...transfer matrix products are computed [deferred] iterations...'. The test case relies on a parameter that is explicitly deferred. A test cannot be constructed to verify 'Lyapunov exponents must yield localization lengths' if the input iteration count is unknown.

Assumption: 'The transfer matrix method will use [deferred] iterations as the default...'. While assumptions can defer values, this specific assumption defines the core execution loop of a primary method (US-2). Deferring the iteration count in the spec prevents the definition of a concrete test procedure for US-2, violating the 'verifiable' check for FR-004/FR-009.

Acceptance Scenario 2: '...standard error ≤ 10% of the mean value'. While measurable, this threshold is arbitrary without a reference to a theoretical baseline or a power analysis justification in the spec. However, it is technically verifiable, so severity is 'writing' rather than 'requirement'.
